Operation 'sendemailtemplate' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks clarity on required inputs (template ID, recipient details), expected outputs (status/confirmation), and side effects (email queuing). Enhanced version specifies what parameters are needed, what is returned, and clarifies that this initiates delivery rather than guaranteeing it. Improved summary to be more specific about the email type.

data.current_description: Send a transactional or automated email using a predefined template with dynamic variable substitution.
data.summary_suggestion: Send transactional email using predefined template
data.description_suggestion: Sends a transactional or automated email using a predefined template with dynamic variable substitution. Accepts template ID, recipient contact information, and variable values for template personalization. Returns a status response confirming message delivery initiation. The email is queued for delivery through the configured email channel.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on required inputs (template ID, recipient details), expected outputs (status/confirmation), and side effects (email queuing). Enhanced version specifies what parameters are needed, what is returned, and clarifies that this initiates delivery rather than guaranteeing it. Improved summary to be more specific about the email type.

message: Operation 'getemailtemplatestats' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is vague about what statistics are returned and what input is required. Enhanced version clarifies the required template ID parameter, specifies the types of metrics returned (send count, open rate, click rate, bounce rate), and explains the use case for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

data.current_description: Retrieve transactional detail and statistics for a specific email template.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves performance statistics and transactional details for a specific email template identified by its ID. Returns metrics such as send count, open rate, click rate, bounce rate, and other engagement statistics. Requires the template ID as a path parameter. Useful for analyzing template effectiveness and campaign performance.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about what statistics are returned and what input is required. Enhanced version clarifies the required template ID parameter, specifies the types of metrics returned (send count, open rate, click rate, bounce rate), and explains the use case for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

message: Operation 'sendwhatsapptemplate' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about required inputs (recipient, template ID or content, variables), expected outputs (status confirmation), and side effects (message queuing, policy compliance). Enhanced version clarifies the two messaging modes (template vs. plain text), specifies required parameters, explains the return value, and notes that delivery is initiated but subject to platform policies. This helps AI agents understand the operation's full scope and limitations.

data.current_description: Send a WhatsApp message using a Meta-approved template or plain text.
data.description_suggestion: Sends a WhatsApp message to a contact using either a Meta-approved template with variable substitution or plain text content. Accepts recipient contact information, template ID or message content, and template variables. Returns a status response confirming message delivery initiation. The message is queued for delivery through WhatsApp channels and subject to Meta's messaging policies.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about required inputs (recipient, template ID or content, variables), expected outputs (status confirmation), and side effects (message queuing, policy compliance). Enhanced version clarifies the two messaging modes (template vs. plain text), specifies required parameters, explains the return value, and notes that delivery is initiated but subject to platform policies. This helps AI agents understand the operation's full scope and limitations.
